frame_label: Other Man's Ownership > Woman's Own Consent
generated_comment: In college, one boy kept trying even after I told him very clearly I was not interested. Only when he heard I was already committed, he stepped back and suddenly became very respectful. That is the reality in many places — a woman's own no is treated lightly, but if she 'belongs' to someone, people immediately understand boundaries.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it normalizes and reports a social logic where a woman's refusal carries less weight than another man's claim over her. It encodes the surface manifestation of stopping pursuit only after learning she is 'taken,' treating male attachment as more legitimate than her own consent.
persona_text: You are Deepa. Age 44. Married. A woman working as a school principal in Chennai. You are giving a rare personal opinion in the staff room to two younger female teachers Ñ one who is 26 and single and one who is 31 and single. They have been talking about their lives and one of them asked you for advice. You are giving it. Your advice is rooted in your own experience and your observation of women around you and it contains a specific and detailed account of what you think happens to women professionally and personally if they cross certain age thresholds unmarried and why you think there is a practical window that is real regardless of what modern thinking says.
